domains.description: L1 index of methodology domains. The retriever reads this first, picks 1-3 candidate domains via the decision-tree, then reads only those domains' INDEX.xml. The disambiguation block defines hard-edges between near-synonym domains.

decision-tree.description: First-match-wins triggers. Phrased from the user's perspective: "user asks about / wants to / is doing X" → route to domain Y.

step.trigger: building an autonomous agent / ReAct / tool-use / multi-agent / planner-executor
prompt engineering, embeddings, RAG basics, LLM fundamentals, model selection
production LLM serving, RAG pipeline, fine-tuning, MLOps, model rollout, eval harness
wiring an AI agent into PR review / CI / kb maintenance / incident triage / governance pipeline
HTTP API, service contract, server-side framework, DB schema, queue, background job
React/Vue/Svelte component, CSS, accessibility, build tooling, state management, design system
general coding craft, testing, code review, refactor, debugging — no specific tier of stack
ADR, service boundary, system design, capacity planning, technology selection, architecture review
CI/CD, deploy, observability, on-call, Kubernetes, terraform, cloud infra, networking, secrets
CVE triage, dependency audit, security-by-design, threat modeling, SOC2/GDPR evidence
requirements elicitation, process model, stakeholder workshop, BPMN, traceability matrix
discovery, JTBD, opportunity sizing, product strategy, launch planning, post-launch review
sprint planning, standup, retro, roadmap, OKR cascade, status report, project ceremonies
UX research synthesis, usability test, user interview, accessibility audit, journey map, persona
market research, competitor scan, survey design, qualitative coding, persona AI-assisted
SEO, content brief, ad copy, growth experiment, funnel, conversion-rate test, launch campaign
status update, demo script, escalation message, contractor brief, difficult conversation
hiring loop, structured interview, candidate experience, 30-60-90 plan, offer negotiation
spec.md / design.md / test-plan.md / implementation-plan.md / SDD ceremony, TASK_NN materialization
Claude Code skill, command, hook, MCP server, plugin authoring, agent file convention

disambiguation.description: Hard edges between near-synonym domains. "If X belongs to A, never to B" — overrides any decision-tree ambiguity.

rule.if: The question is stack-agnostic (refactor, test, code review, debugging discipline)
The question is about a stack the user touches via HTTP server-side (DB, API, queue, auth)
The question is about what the end-user sees and interacts with in a browser / native UI
"How does this model think / what is a token / which model do I pick" type question
The artefact under design IS an agent (tool-use loop, planner, memory)
The artefact is production AI infra (serving, batching, eval, RAG pipeline plumbing)
The agent is plumbed into the SDLC (PR review bot, kb maintainer, incident triager)
The user produces ceremonies / dashboards / status / sprint artefacts
The user decides what to build and for whom (discovery, opportunity, JTBD, launch)
The user shapes requirements, models processes, traces requirements across the lifecycle
Activity produces a design / journey map / accessibility audit / usability test
Activity produces persona / market understanding / interview analysis without a design output
Decision is about long-lived shape (ADR, service boundary, technology choice)
Decision is about runtime operation (deploy, observe, scale, recover)
Activity creates a written contract that grounds LLM code generation (spec/design/test-plan)
Activity is a project-management ceremony or status-tracking ritual
Question is about a specific vulnerability / dependency / CVE / threat model
Question is about who can call what, network ACL, secret rotation cadence
Question is about whether to centralise/distribute auth, where the trust boundary lives

domain.scope: AI agents and agentic systems — agent architectures, tool use, multi-agent orchestration, memory, planning, evaluation, production-grade agent patterns.

typical-asks.ask: How do I make my agent stop and ask the human?
How do I evaluate whether my agent's tool calls are correct?
How do I structure a planner-executor split?
How do I protect my agent from prompt injection at the tool boundary?

domain.scope: Core AI / ML foundations — model selection, prompt engineering basics, embeddings, RAG fundamentals, LLM integration patterns, primitives that AI-powered features are built from.

typical-asks.ask: When do I pick Opus vs Sonnet vs Haiku?
How do I structure a prompt that an agent will follow reliably?
What's the cheapest way to add semantic search to my app?
How do I write evals that catch silent regression?

domain.scope: Software architecture — ADRs, API gateway patterns, service boundaries, reversibility tagging, observability design, software-architect decisions that shape long-lived systems.

typical-asks.ask: Should I split this monolith into services now?
How do I write an ADR a future-me will actually use?
Where does this new responsibility belong in the system?
How do I make my architecture diagram match the running code?

domain.scope: Methodologies in the automation-tooling domain.

Business analysis — requirements elicitation, process modeling, stakeholder management, KPI design, change-request impact analysis, BA core practice + modeling techniques bridging product strategy and delivery.

typical-asks.ask: How do I elicit requirements from a stakeholder who keeps changing their mind?
How do I draw a BPMN that engineers will read?
How do I run a definition-of-ready check on a feature?
How do I trace a regulatory clause to a test case?

domain.scope: Backend engineering — APIs, services, databases, server-side patterns across Python/Node/Java/Rails/PHP/.NET, including auth, caching, queues, background work, contract-first design and enterprise framework patterns.

typical-asks.ask: How do I shape an API contract before writing the handler?
How do I rotate secrets without downtime?
How do I model soft-deletes in my Django app?
How do I make my background jobs idempotent?

domain.scope: Claude Code authoring stack — agents, commands, hooks, skills, MCP servers, project documentation conventions for building reliable agentic tooling on top of Claude.

typical-asks.ask: How do I write a Claude Code skill that auto-invokes correctly?
How do I add an MCP server for my custom tool?
How do I structure project AGENTS.md for multi-agent compatibility?

domain.scope: Communication craft for solopreneurs and small agencies — stakeholder updates, executive demos, contractor and freelancer scripts, negotiation, storytelling, difficult conversations, customer-facing testimonials.

typical-asks.ask: How do I write a status report stakeholders actually read?
How do I escalate without setting fire to the relationship?
How do I script a difficult conversation with a contractor?

domain.scope: Software engineering practice — coding craft, code quality, testing, devtools. The dev bucket covers anything a software developer does day-to-day that's stack-agnostic.

typical-asks.ask: How do I refactor without breaking everything?
How do I write a test that's actually useful?
How do I review a PR without being a jerk?
How do I unflake a slow test suite?

domain.scope: Frontend and UI engineering — design systems, component libraries, accessibility, performance, state management, build tooling, UI/UX implementation patterns for web and spatial interfaces.

typical-asks.ask: How do I keep my design system honest as components grow?
How do I make a React form that handles validation cleanly?
How do I ship an a11y fix that doesn't regress?

domain.scope: HR and recruiting practice — full-cycle hiring, structured interview design, STAR method, candidate experience, employer branding, EVP, 30-60-90 onboarding, retention compliance.

typical-asks.ask: How do I run a structured interview that beats gut feel?
How do I design a 30-60-90 plan a new hire can actually complete?
How do I write a job posting that filters in, not out?

domain.scope: Infrastructure and platform engineering — DevOps, CI/CD pipelines, observability, server craft, cloud, networking, deploy and operate everything that runs the code in production.

typical-asks.ask: How do I add tracing without rewriting half the codebase?
How do I deploy with confidence at 6pm Friday? (you don't, but how do you make Monday safer)
How do I structure a runbook the on-call will actually read at 2am?
How do I get my CI from 18 minutes to under 5?

domain.scope: Methodologies in the llm-integration domain.
Marketing practice — content, SEO, growth, PPC, SMM, CRO, GTM. Everything that drives demand and converts it into customers.

typical-asks.ask: How do I pick a single distribution channel for my first 100 users?
How do I write a content brief from search intent?
How do I run a CRO experiment that beats noise?
How do I script a launch campaign that doesn't peter out at day three?

domain.scope: Applied ML / AI engineering practice — building, evaluating, deploying, operating LLM and ML systems: RAG pipelines, agentic patterns, eval-driven development, fine-tuning, prompt engineering, model rollout, MLOps for production AI features.

typical-asks.ask: How do I stand up a RAG pipeline that beats vanilla vector search?
How do I migrate from one LLM provider to another without breaking eval?
How do I run an eval harness in CI?
How do I cut inference cost 40% without quality regression?

domain.scope: Product + project management — planning, prioritization, stakeholder management, agile/traditional ceremonies, ops, KPIs, roadmap delivery.

typical-asks.ask: How do I run a 15-minute standup that surfaces blockers, not status?
How do I cascade an OKR from company to team without ritual theatre?
How do I write a weekly status that the CEO actually opens?
How do I refresh my risk register in under an hour?

domain.scope: Product management discipline — discovery, opportunity sizing, product strategy, launch planning, post-launch review, product operations, account health.

typical-asks.ask: How do I decide what to build next when everything looks important?
How do I size an opportunity without making up numbers?
How do I run a kill review on a stalled product line?
How do I plan a launch that doesn't disappoint at week two?

domain.scope: Research practice — user research, market research, qualitative coding protocols, AI-assisted persona building, interview analysis, research synthesis.

typical-asks.ask: How do I code 30 interview transcripts without losing the plot?
How do I size a market without paying for a Gartner report?
How do I build a persona from data, not vibes?

domain.scope: Spec-driven development — constitution, roadmap, spec, design, test plan, implementation plan, ADR consequence binding, AI-assisted spec writing, the full SDD lifecycle from backlog to done.

typical-asks.ask: How do I write a spec.md an agent can implement without hallucination?
How do I materialise tasks from an implementation-plan?
How do I run an SDD batch through a subagent pool?
How do I know my design.md is ready to leave todo/?

domain.scope: Wiring AI agents into the SDLC — lang/lint/test/tracker/kb/task/mr/inc/sec/gov methodologies that connect agents to the development pipeline floor: PR review, kb maintenance, incident triage, governance.

typical-asks.ask: How do I wire an AI PR reviewer that doesn't drown the team in noise?
How do I let an agent maintain our internal kb without it hallucinating decisions?
How do I make my agent handle an incident triage handoff?

domain.scope: Application and supply-chain security — CVE triage, dependency audit cadence, threat modeling, repeatable security artefacts for solo and small-team development.

typical-asks.ask: How do I triage a CVE in under 30 minutes?
How do I set up dependency-audit cadence that doesn't get ignored?

domain.scope: UX design and research practice — UI design, UX research, user research, accessibility specialist work, design ops, usability and conversion design.

typical-asks.ask: How do I run a usability test on a prototype in one sitting?
How do I audit a single screen for accessibility?
How do I turn a journey map into a backlog?
How do I write a heuristic eval that a stakeholder can argue with?
